segments.text: When I had finished speaking these words, the angel which had been sent to me the nights before was sent to me.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: He said to me, {
segments.speaker: Uriel

segments.text: Rise, Esdras, and hear the words that I have come to tell you.
segments.speaker: Uriel

segments.text: I said, {
segments.speaker: Esdras

segments.text: Speak on, my Lord.
segments.speaker: Uriel

segments.text: Then he said to me,
segments.speaker: Uriel

segments.text: There is a sea set in a wide place, that it might be broad and vast,
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: but its entrance is set in a narrow place so as to be like a river.
segments.speaker: Uriel

segments.text: Whoever desires to go into the sea to look at it, or to rule it, if he didn’t go through the narrow entrance, how could he come into the broad part?
segments.speaker: Esdras

segments.text: Another thing also: There is a city built and set in a plain country, and full of all good things,
segments.speaker: Esdras

segments.text: but its entrance is narrow, and is set in a dangerous place to fall, having fire on the right hand, and deep water on the left.
segments.speaker: Uriel

segments.text: There is one only path between them both, even between the fire and the water, so that only one person can go there at once.
segments.speaker: Uriel

segments.text: If this city is now given to a man for an inheritance, if the heir doesn’t pass the danger before him, how will he receive his inheritance?
segments.speaker: Uriel

segments.text: I said, {
segments.speaker: Uriel

segments.text: That is so, Lord.
segments.speaker: Uriel

segments.text: Then said he to me,
Even so also is Israel’s portion.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: I made the world for their sakes. What is now done was decreed when Adam transgressed my statutes.
segments.speaker: Uriel

segments.text: Then the entrances of this world were made narrow, sorrowful, and toilsome. They are but few and evil, full of perils, and involved in great toils.
segments.speaker: Esdras

segments.text: For the entrances of the greater world are wide and safe, and produce fruit of immortality.
segments.speaker: Uriel

segments.text: So if those who live don’t enter these difficult and vain things, they can never receive those that are reserved for them.
segments.speaker: Uriel

segments.text: Now therefore why are you disturbed, seeing you are but a corruptible man? Why are you moved, since you are mortal?
segments.speaker: Uriel

segments.text: Why haven’t you considered in your mind that which is to come, rather than that which is present?
segments.speaker: Phaltiel

segments.text: Then I answered and said, {
segments.speaker: Phaltiel

segments.text: O sovereign Lord, behold, you have ordained in your law that the righteous will inherit these things, but that the ungodly will perish.
segments.speaker: Esdras

segments.text: The righteous therefore will suffer difficult things, and hope for easier things, but those who have done wickedly have suffered the difficult things, and yet will not see the easier things.
segments.speaker: Phaltiel

segments.text: He said to me, {
segments.speaker: Esdras

segments.text: You are not a judge above God, neither do you have more understanding than the Most High.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Yes, let many perish who now live, rather than that the law of God which is set before them be despised.
segments.speaker: Uriel

segments.text: For God strictly commanded those who came, even as they came, what they should do to live, and what they should observe to avoid punishment.
segments.speaker: Uriel

segments.text: Nevertheless, they weren’t obedient to him, but spoke against him and imagined for themselves vain things.
segments.speaker: Uriel

segments.text: They made cunning plans of wickedness, and said moreover of the Most High that he doesn’t exist, and they didn’t know his ways.
segments.speaker: Uriel

segments.text: They despised his law and denied his covenants. They haven’t been faithful to his statutes, and haven’t performed his works.
segments.speaker: Uriel

segments.text: Therefore, Esdras, for the empty are empty things, and for the full are the full things.
segments.speaker: Uriel

segments.text: For behold, the time will come, and it will be, when these signs of which I told you before will come to pass, that the bride will appear, even the city coming forth, and she will be seen who now is withdrawn from the earth.
segments.speaker: Uriel

segments.text: Whoever is delivered from the foretold evils will see my wonders.
segments.speaker: Uriel

segments.text: For my son Jesus will be revealed with those who are with him, and those who remain will rejoice four hundred years.
segments.speaker: Uriel

segments.text: After these years my son Christ will die, along with all of those who have the breath of life.
segments.speaker: Uriel

segments.text: Then the world will be turned into the old silence seven days, like as in the first beginning, so that no human will remain.
segments.speaker: Uriel

segments.text: After seven days the world that is not yet awake will be raised up, and what is corruptible will die.
segments.speaker: Uriel

segments.text: The earth will restore those who are asleep in it, and the dust those who dwell in it in silence, and the secret places will deliver those souls that were committed to them.
segments.speaker: Uriel

segments.text: The Most High will be revealed on the judgment seat,  and compassion will pass away, and patience will be withdrawn.
segments.speaker: Esdras

segments.text: Only judgment will remain. Truth will st and. Faith will grow strong.
segments.speaker: Uriel

segments.text: Recompense will follow. The reward will be shown. Good deeds will awake, and wicked deeds won’t sleep.
segments.speaker: Uriel

segments.text: The pit of torment will appear, and near it will be the place of rest. The furnace of hell will be shown, and near it the paradise of delight.
segments.speaker: Uriel

segments.text: Then the Most High will say to the nations that are raised from the dead, ‘Look and understand whom you have denied, whom you haven’t served, whose commandments you have despised.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Look on this side and on that. Here is delight and rest, and there fire and torments.’ Thus he will speak to them in the day of judgment.
segments.speaker: Esdras

segments.text: This is a day that has neither sun, nor moon, nor stars,
segments.speaker: Esdras

segments.text: neither cloud, nor thunder, nor lightning, neither wind, nor water, nor air, neither darkness, nor evening, nor morning,
segments.speaker: Esdras

segments.text: neither summer, nor spring, nor heat, nor winter, neither frost, nor cold, nor hail, nor rain, nor dew,
segments.speaker: Uriel

segments.text: neither noon, nor night, nor dawn, neither shining, nor brightness, nor light, except only the splendor of the glory of the Most High, by which all will see the things that are set before them.
segments.speaker: Uriel

segments.text: It will endure as though it were a week of years.
segments.speaker: Uriel

segments.text: This is my judgment and its prescribed order; but I have only shown these things to you.
segments.speaker: Uriel

segments.text: I answered, {
I said then, O Lord, and I say now: Blessed are those who are now alive and keep your commandments!
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: But what about those for whom I prayed? For who is there of those who are alive who has not sinned, and who of the children of men hasn’t transgressed your covenant?
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Now I see that the world to come will bring delight to few, but torments to many.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: For an evil heart has grown up in us, which has led us astray from these commandments and has brought us into corruption and into the ways of death. It has shown us the paths of perdition and removed us far from life—and that, not a few only, but nearly all who have been created.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: He answered me, {
Listen to me, and I will instruct you. I will admonish you yet again.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: For this reason, the Most High has not made one world, but two.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: For because you have said that the just are not many, but few, and the ungodly abound, hear the explanation.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: If you have just a few precious stones, will you add them to lead and clay?
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: I said, {
Lord, how could that be?
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: He said to me, {
Not only that, but ask the earth, and she will tell you. Defer to her, and she will declare it to you.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Say to her, ‘You produce gold, silver, and brass, and also iron, lead, and clay;
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: but silver is more abundant than gold, and brass than silver, and iron than brass, and lead than iron, and clay than lead.’
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Judge therefore which things are precious and to be desired, what is abundant or what is rare?
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: I said, {
O sovereign Lord, that which is plentiful is of less worth, for that which is more rare is more precious.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: He answered me, {
Weigh within yourself the things that you have thought, for he who has what is hard to get rejoices over him who has what is plentiful.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: So also is the judgment which I have promised; for I will rejoice over the few that will be saved, because these are those who have made my glory to prevail now, and through them, my name is now honored.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: I won’t grieve over the multitude of those who perish; for these are those who are now like mist, and have become like flame and smoke; they are set on fire and burn hotly, and are extinguished.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: I answered, {
O earth, why have you produced, if the mind is made out of dust, like all other created things?
segments.speaker: Esdras

segments.text: For it would have been better that the dust itself had been unborn, so that the mind might not have been made from it.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: But now the mind grows with us, and because of this we are tormented, because we perish and we know it.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Let the race of men lament and the animals of the field be glad. Let all who are born lament, but let the four-footed animals and the livestock rejoice.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: For it is far better with them than with us; for they don’t look forward to judgment, neither do they know of torments or of salvation promised to them after death.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: For what does it profit us, that we will be preserved alive, but yet be afflicted with torment?
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: For all who are born are defiled with iniquities, and are full of sins and laden with transgressions.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: If after death we were not to come into judgment, perhaps it would have been better for us.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: He answered me, {
When the Most High made the world and Adam and all those who came from him, he first prepared the judgment and the things that pertain to the judgment.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Now understand from your own words, for you have said that the mind grows with us.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: They therefore who dwell on the earth will be tormented for this reason, that having understanding they have committed iniquity, and receiving commandments have not kept them, and having obtained a law they dealt unfaithfully with that which they received.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: What then will they have to say in the judgment, or how will they answer in the last times?
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: For how long a time has the Most High been patient with those who inhabit the world, and not for their sakes, but because of the times which he has foreordained!
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: I answered, {

If I have found grace in your sight, O Lord, show this also to your servant, whether after death, even now when every one of us gives up his soul, we will be kept in rest until those times come, in which you renew the creation, or whether we will be tormented immediately.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: He answered me, {
I will show you this also; but don’t join yourself with those who are scorners, nor count yourself with those who are tormented.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: For you have a treasure of works laid up with the Most High, but it won’t be shown you until the last times.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: For concerning death the teaching is: When the decisive sentence has gone out from the Most High that a man shall die, as the spirit leaves the body to return again to him who gave it, it adores the glory of the Most High first of all.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: And if it is one of those who have been scorners and have not kept the way of the Most High, and that have despised his law, and who hate those who fear God,
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: these spirits won’t enter into habitations, but will wander and be in torments immediately, ever grieving and sad, in seven ways.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: The first way, because they have despised the law of the Most High.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: The second way, because they can’t now make a good repentance that they may live.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: The third way, they will see the reward laid up for those who have believed the covenants of the Most High.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: The fourth way, they will consider the torment laid up for themselves in the last days.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: The fifth way, they will see the dwelling places of the others guarded by angels, with great quietness.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: The sixth way, they will see how immediately some of them will pass into torment.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: The seventh way, which is more grievous than all the aforesaid ways, because they will pine away in confusion and be consumed with shame, and will be withered up by fears, seeing the glory of the Most High before whom they have sinned while living, and before whom they will be judged in the last times.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: “Now this is the order of those who have kept the ways of the Most High, when they will be separated from their mortal body.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: In the time that they lived in it, they painfully served the Most High, and were in jeopardy every hour, that they might keep the law of the lawgiver perfectly.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Therefore this is the teaching concerning them:
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: First of all they will see with great joy the glory of him who takes them up, for they will have rest in seven orders.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: The first order, because they have labored with great effort to overcome the evil thought which was fashioned together with them, that it might not lead them astray from life into death.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: The second order, because they see the perplexity in which the souls of the ungodly wander, and the punishment that awaits them.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: The third order, they see the testimony which he who fashioned them gives concerning them, that while they lived they kept the law which was given them in trust.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: The fourth order, they understand the rest which, being gathered in their chambers, they now enjoy with great quietness, guarded by angels, and the glory that awaits them in the last days.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: The fifth order, they rejoice that they have now escaped from that which is corruptible, and that they will inherit that which is to come, while they see in addition the difficulty and the pain from which they have been delivered, and the spacious liberty which they will receive with joy and immortality.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: The sixth order, when it is shown to them how their face will shine like the sun, and how they will be made like the light of the stars, being incorruptible from then on.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: The seventh order, which is greater than all the previously mentioned orders, because they will rejoice with confidence, and because they will be bold without confusion, and will be glad without fear, for they hurry to see the face of him whom in their lifetime they served, and from whom they will receive their reward in glory.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: This is the order of the souls of the just, as from henceforth is announced to them. Previously mentioned are the ways of torture which those who would not give heed will suffer from after this.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: I answered, {
Will time therefore be given to the souls after they are separated from the bodies, that they may see what you have described to me?
segments.speaker: Esdras

segments.text: He said, {
Their freedom will be for seven days, that for seven days they may see the things you have been told, and afterwards they will be gathered together in their habitations.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: I answered, {
If I have found favor in your sight, show further to me your servant whether in the day of judgment the just will be able to intercede for the ungodly or to entreat the Most High for them,
segments.speaker: Esdras

segments.text: whether fathers for children, or children for parents, or kindred for kindred, or kinsfolk for their next of kin, or friends for those who are most dear.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: He answered me, {

Since you have found favor in my sight, I will show you this also. The day of judgment is a day of decision, and displays to all the seal of truth. Even as now a father doesn’t send his son, or a son his father, or a master his slave, or a friend him that is most dear, that in his place he may understand, or sleep, or eat, or be healed,
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: so no one will ever pray for another in that day, neither will one lay a burden on another, for then everyone will each bear his own righteousness or unrighteousness.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: I answered, {
How do we now find that first Abraham prayed for the people of Sodom, and Moses for the ancestors who sinned in the wilderness,
segments.speaker: Esdras

segments.text: and Joshua after him for Israel in the days of Achan,
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: and Samuel in the days of Saul, and David for the plague, and Solomon for those who would worship in the sanctuary,
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: and Elijah for those that received rain, and for the dead, that he might live,
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: and Hezekiah for the people in the days of Sennacherib, and many others prayed for many?
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: If therefore now, when corruption has grown and unrighteousness increased, the righteous have prayed for the ungodly, why will it not be so then also?
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: He answered me, {
This present world is not the end. The full glory doesn’t remain in it. Therefore those who were able prayed for the weak.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: But the day of judgment will be the end of this age, and the beginning of the immortality to come, in which corruption has passed away,
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: intemperance is at an end, infidelity is cut off, but righteousness has grown, and truth has sprung up.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Then no one will be able to have mercy on him who is condemned in judgment, nor to harm someone who is victorious.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: I answered then, {
This is my first and last saying, that it would have been better if the earth had not produced Adam, or else, when it had produced him, to have restrained him from sinning.
segments.speaker: Esdras

segments.text: For what profit is it for all who are in this present time to live in heaviness, and after death to look for punishment?
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: O Adam, what have you done? For though it was you who sinned, the evil hasn’t fallen on you alone, but on all of us who come from you.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: For what profit is it to us, if an immortal time is promised to us, but we have done deeds that bring death?
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: And that there is promised us an everlasting hope, but we have most miserably failed?
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: And that there are reserved habitations of health and safety, but we have lived wickedly?
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: And that the glory of the Most High will defend those who have led a pure life, but we have walked in the most wicked ways of all?
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: And that a paradise will be revealed, whose fruit endures without decay, in which is abundance and healing, but we won’t enter into it,
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: for we have lived in perverse ways?
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: And that the faces of those who have practiced self-control will shine more than the stars, but our faces will be blacker than darkness?
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: For while we lived and committed iniquity, we didn’t consider what we would have to suffer after death.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Then he answered, {
This is the significance of the battle which humans born on the earth will fight:
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: if they are overcome, they will suffer as you have said, but if they get the victory, they will receive the thing that I say.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: For this is the way that Moses spoke to the people while he lived, saying, ‘Choose life, that you may live!’
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Nevertheless they didn’t believe him or the prophets after him, not even me, who have spoken to them.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Therefore there won’t be such heaviness in their destruction, as there will be joy over those who are assured of salvation.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Then I answered, {
I know, Lord, that the Most High is now called merciful, in that he has mercy upon those who have not yet come into the world;
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: and compassionate, in that he has compassion upon those who turn to his law;
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: and patient, in that he is patient with those who have sinned, since they are his creatures;
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: and bountiful, in that he is ready to give rather than to take away;
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: and very merciful, in that he multiplies more and more mercies to those who are present, and who are past, and also to those who are to come—
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: for if he wasn’t merciful, the world wouldn’t continue with those who dwell in it—
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: and one who forgives, for if he didn’t forgive out of his goodness, that those who have committed iniquities might be relieved of them, not even one ten thousandth part of mankind would remain living;
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: and a judge, for if he didn’t pardon those who were created by his word, and blot out the multitude of sins,
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: there would perhaps be very few left of an innumerable multitude.
segments.speaker: Unknown
